Acceptance complete for HXC-SCAF-003, HXC-SCAF-007, and HXC-SEM-006 target-identity validation. The experiment also proved a material pin constraint: real Haxe 4.3.7 Cross predefines target.name=cross and unremovable target.utf16/utf16 before macros. ADR 0004/0007, PRD, architecture, configuration, toolchain, research, README, AGENTS, diagnostic catalog, CI, pre-commit, and live downstream notes agree that Eval is a test carrier only and its sys/threaded facts are not C capability claims. ADR 0007, ADR 0004, PRD, README, AGENTS, architecture, configuration, toolchain, provenance, research, and checked plan now agree. Rejected Haxe 4 Cross because unremovable UTF-16 facts leak to application conditionals; rejected a Haxe 4 fork because of cross-host distribution/maintenance burden; retained Eval only for future hxc bootstrap, differential oracle, and isolation. Exact official platform artifact hashes are lock-checked. Completed E1.T07 and HXC-COMP-009/011/012 with a typed filesystem-independent CProjectEmissionPlan, closed artifact/configuration variants, validated SHA-256 GeneratedFile values, deterministic UTF-8 ordering, merged build-fact provenance, finalized symbol/runtime/ABI/stdlib/manifest sidecars, and a fail-closed ReflaxeOutputWriter. Completed E2.T01 and HXC-SEM-001/003/005. Added accepted ADR 0008 plus the schema-validated primitive semantics contract; typed Haxe primitive mapping and HxcIR schema 2 conversion/nullability forms; deterministic portable/metal representation rules; strict tests for widening, narrowing, signed and unsigned behavior, NaN, infinities, negative zero, overflow, and nullable values. Completed E2.T06 in commit cd6bb19. The compiler now lowers typed Haxe if expressions and statements, exact lazy Boolean operators, while and do-while loops, pinned Int range loops, Int statement and value switches, break, and continue into explicit ordered HxcIR blocks and edges before structural C emission. Switch subjects are evaluated once, every C case and default transfers explicitly, nested loop targets are lexical and deterministic, and value-switch carriers are initialized only after a structural proof that every arm assigns the same typed local. Unsupported throw and unreachable statements retain source-positioned stable diagnostics instead of being guessed or emitted as raw C. Added deterministic HxcIR/C/symbol snapshots, validator negatives, Eval differential execution, nested break and continue coverage, exact short-circuit side-effect checks, optimized native execution, profile comparison, runtime-none proof, fixture taxonomy records, and updated architecture and lowering documentation. Pattern, enum, String, Float, and fallthrough switches, arbitrary iterators, cleanup-bearing exits, and exception lowering remain deliberately fail-closed for their owning future work.

Completed HXC-SEM-019, HXC-RT-005, and HXC-RT-011 in implementation commit dce97ac. The provisional allocator now has checked size arithmetic; explicit power-of-two alignment; canonical zero-size behavior; failure-atomic allocate/resize out-parameters; allocator-identity comparison; and an owned hxc_allocation record whose allocator follows move, resize, and disposal across boundaries. Hosted over-alignment is portable and freestanding builds require an explicit allocator. Executable evidence covers overflow, invalid and over-alignment, zero-size, OOM/reallocation preservation, callback misalignment, cross-boundary ownership, and exact release identity in runtime/hxrt/test/allocator_contract.c and runtime_smoke.c. scripts/ci/runtime_smoke.py proves the freestanding allocator object has no unresolved libc allocation symbols, runs a static-arena custom allocator, runs Clang AddressSanitizer plus UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer, and links C layout facts into a C++17 public-header consumer. The governed ABI case is test/abi/allocator-contract/case.json and the full contract is docs/allocator-abi.md. Verification passed with npm test, scripts/hooks/pre-commit, npm run snapshots:check, npm run test:runtime-features, npm run test:fixture-policy, npm run test:capabilities, npm run test:typed-boundaries, npm run format:haxe:check, python3 scripts/ci/check_ci_policy.py, and git diff --check.
